name: Constellation Energy
rating_source: Claude fundamental analysis based on official Constellation Energy 10-K (FY2025), Q4 2025 earnings release, 2026 business outlook presentation, and IR filings. Wall Street consensus: Strong Buy (16 Buy, 3 Hold, 1 Sell out of 20 analysts). Average 12-month price target: $381. Assessed May 2026.
thesis: World's largest private power producer (55 GW post-Calpine) with the nation's premier nuclear fleet (22 GW, 183 TWh zero-emissions generation in 2025). FY2025 revenue $25.5B (+8% YoY) with adjusted EPS of $9.39. Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026 for $16.4B, adding 23 GW of natural gas and geothermal generation, promising 20%+ EPS accretion. Guided 2026 adjusted EPS to $11.00-12.00 with 20% CAGR commitment through 2029. Key datacenter PPAs: Microsoft (Crane Clean Energy Center restart, 20-year PPA), Meta (Clinton nuclear, 1.1 GW), CyrusOne (380 MW). Stock down ~13% YTD as guidance disappointed vs Street expectations, creating potential entry point. $5B share buyback authorized.
catalysts: Calpine integration delivering 20%+ EPS accretion — Q1 2026 EBITDA consensus $2.02B (+139% sequential)
2026 adjusted EPS guidance $11-12 with 20% CAGR commitment through 2029
Crane Clean Energy Center restart (835 MW) backed by 20-year Microsoft PPA
Nuclear license renewals: Clinton extended to 2047, Dresden to 2049/2051 — $370M invested
$5B share buyback program authorized — significant capital return
risks: Crane Clean Energy Center interconnection flagged for potential delay to 2031 by PJM — threatens Microsoft PPA
Calpine integration complexity: 2,300 employees, 60+ power plants, rising interest expense from $16.4B financing
2026 guidance midpoint ($11.50 EPS) below initial Street consensus ($11.73) — sent stock down 7%
Nuclear operational risk across 22 GW fleet — unplanned outages impact earnings
Regulatory and policy risk: clean energy subsidies, capacity market reforms, PJM interconnection rules

name: Vertiv
rating_source: Claude fundamental analysis based on official Vertiv 10-K (FY2025), Q1 2026 earnings release (April 22, 2026), and IR filings. Wall Street consensus: Strong Buy (22 Buy, 3 Hold, 0 Sell out of 25 analysts). Average 12-month price target: $339. Assessed May 2026.

Net leverage at 0.2x with $5.0B liquidity. CoolPhase Flex hybrid cooling and 800VDC power solutions position Vertiv at the center of the liquid cooling transition for 100+ kW AI racks.

name: FuelCell Energy
rating_source: Claude fundamental analysis based on official FuelCell Energy 10-K (FY2025 ended Oct 31, 2025), Q1 FY2026 earnings release, and IR filings. Wall Street consensus: Hold (6 analysts, avg PT $8.24). Assessed May 2026.
thesis: Carbonate fuel cell manufacturer pivoting aggressively to datacenter power. Q1 FY2026 revenue $30.5M (+61% YoY). 1.5 GW datacenter pipeline with 275% growth since Feb 2025. New 12.5 MW standardized power blocks for rapid datacenter deployment. SDCL partnership for 450 MW identified projects. Tri-gen technology produces electricity, hydrogen, and heat (absorption cooling for DC thermal management). Still pre-profit with $192M operating loss in FY2025, but $379.6M cash provides runway.
catalysts: Datacenter pipeline 1.5+ GW with 275% growth — SDCL partnership for 450 MW
New 12.5 MW standardized power block for rapid datacenter deployment (months, not years)
Q1 FY2026 revenue +61% YoY to $30.5M — accelerating growth
Tri-gen technology: electricity + hydrogen + absorption cooling for DC thermal loads
Manufacturing expansion planned: 100 MW to 350 MW capacity at Torrington, CT
risks: $1.17B backlog declining 10.8% — revenue recognized faster than new awards
Still deeply unprofitable: $192M operating loss in FY2025, net loss $7.42/share
Dilution risk: $56.3M raised via at-the-market stock sales in Q1 2026
Small revenue base ($158M FY2025) relative to datacenter power ambitions
Competition from Bloom Energy, natural gas generators, and grid power
